segments.text: How long wilt thou mourn for Saul, seeing I have rejected him from reigning over Israel? fill thine horn with oil, and go, I will send thee to Jesse the Beth-lehemite: for I have provided me a king among his sons.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: How can I go? if Saul hear it, he will kill me.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Take an heifer with thee, and say, I am come to sacrifice to the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

footnotes: with thee: Heb. in thine hand

segments.text: And call Jesse to the sacrifice, and I will shew thee what thou shalt do: and thou shalt anoint unto me him whom I name unto thee.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And Samuel did that which the LORD spake, and came to Beth-lehem.
And the elders of the town trembled at his coming,
Comest thou peaceably?
segments.speaker: Elders of the town

segments.text: Peaceably: I am come to sacrifice unto the LORD: sanctify yourselves, and come with me to the sacrifice.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he sanctified Jesse and his sons, and called them to the sacrifice.
And it came to pass, when they were come, that he looked on Eliab,
Surely the LORD's anointed is before him.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Look not on his countenance, or on the height of his stature; because I have refused him: for the Lord seeth not as man seeth; for man looketh on the outward appearance, but the LORD looketh on the heart.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then Jesse called Abinadab, and made him pass before Samuel.
Neither hath the LORD chosen this.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Then Jesse made Shammah to pass by.
Neither hath the LORD chosen this.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Again, Jesse made seven of his sons to pass before Samuel.
The LORD hath not chosen these.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Are here all thy children?
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: There remaineth yet the youngest, and, behold, he keepeth the sheep.
segments.speaker: Jesse

segments.text: Send and fetch him: for we will not sit down till he come hither.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And he sent, and brought him in. Now he was ruddy, and withal of a beautiful countenance, and goodly to look to.
Arise, anoint him: for this is he.
segments.speaker: The LORD

footnotes: of a…: Heb. fair of eyes

segments.text: Then Samuel took the horn of oil, and anointed him in the midst of his brethren: and the Spirit of the LORD came upon David from that day forward. So Samuel rose up, and went to Ramah.
But the Spirit of the LORD departed from Saul, and an evil spirit from the LORD troubled him.
Behold now, an evil spirit from God troubleth thee.
segments.speaker: Saul's servants

segments.text: Let our lord now command thy servants, which are before thee, to seek out a man, who is a cunning player on an harp: and it shall come to pass, when the evil spirit from God is upon thee, that he shall play with his hand, and thou shalt be well.
segments.speaker: Saul's servants

segments.text: Provide me now a man that can play well, and bring him to me.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Behold, I have seen a son of Jesse the Beth-lehemite, that is cunning in playing, and a mighty valiant man, and a man of war, and prudent in matters, and a comely person, and the LORD is with him.
segments.speaker: One of Saul's servants

segments.text: Wherefore Saul sent messengers unto Jesse, and said, Send me David thy son, which is with the sheep.
And Jesse took an ass laden with bread, and a bottle of wine, and a kid, and sent them by David his son unto Saul.
And David came to Saul, and stood before him: and he loved him greatly; and he became his armourbearer.
And Saul sent to Jesse, saying, Let David, I pray thee, stand before me; for he hath found favour in my sight.
And it came to pass, when the evil spirit from God was upon Saul, that David took an harp, and played with his hand: so Saul was refreshed, and was well, and the evil spirit departed from him.
